answer: ValidationError is used when input data fails structural or format validation (e.g. a required field is missing, a string exceeds max length) — typically caught at the application or domain boundary before business rules are applied. InvalidDataError is used when the data is structurally valid but violates a business rule or domain invariant (e.g. an end date is before a start date). ValidationError maps to HTTP 400 with validation details; InvalidDataError also maps to HTTP 400/422 but signals a semantic domain violation rather than a format problem.
